blocks:
Sūrat al-Humazah

Meccan, and it consists of nine verses by consensus (بالإِجماع)* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)** وَيْلٌ لِكُلِّ هُمَزَةٍ لُمَزَةٍ ** (1) Woe to every backbiter, slanderer,

The origin of الهمز is breaking (الكسر), and اللمز is slandering (الطّعن), and they became widespread in the context of breaking reputations (الأعراض) through slandering (بالطّعن).

Al-Qummi said: هُمَزَةٍ ** is the one who disparages people (يغمز الناس) and holds the poor in contempt (ويستحقر الفقراء). And His saying لُمَزَةٍ ** is the one who twists his neck and his head (يلوي عنقه ورأسه) and becomes angry (ويغضب) when he sees a poor person (فقيراً) or a beggar (سائلاً).

(٢) الَّذِي جَمَعَ مالاً وَعَدَّدَهُ ** (2) who amasses wealth and counts it over. And made it a provision (عدّة) for calamities (للنّوازل), or counted it repeatedly (عدّة مرّة بعد أخرى). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: He prepared it (أعدّه) and stored it (وضعه). And جمع was read with tashdid (بالتّشديد) to denote abundance (للتكثير). (٣) يَحْسَبُ أَنَّ مالَهُ أَخْلَدَهُ ** (3) He supposes that his wealth will immortalize him. That it leaves him immortal (خالداً) in the world. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: And keeps him (ويبقيه). (٤) كَلَّا لَيُنْبَذَنَ فِي الْحُطَمَةِ ** (4) No indeed! He will surely be cast into the Crusher. He will surely be cast (ليطرحنّ). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: The Fire (النّار) which crushes (تحطم) everything. (٥) وَما أَدْراكَ مَا الْحُطَمَةُ. (5) And what will show you what the Crusher is? (٦) نارُ اللهِ الْمُوقَدَةُ ** (6) The Fire of Allah, kindled, Which Allah has kindled (أو قدها). And what Allah has kindled, none other than Him is able to extinguish it. (٧) الَّتِي تَطَّلِعُ عَلَى الْأَفْئِدَةِ ** (7) which rises above the hearts. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It blazes (تتلهّب) upon the heart (على الفؤاد). (٨) إِنَّها عَلَيْهِمْ مُؤْصَدَةٌ ** (8) It will be sealed upon them, He said: Sealed shut (مطبقة). (٩) فِي عَمَدٍ مُمَدَّدَةٍ ** (9) in extended columns. In extended pillars (في اعمد ممدودة), meaning: they are bound (موثّقين) in extended pillars. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: When the pillars (العمد) are extended over them, by Allah, that is eternity (الخلود). And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشي) narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that which is similar in meaning. And عُمد was read with two dammahs (بضمّتين). In Thawab al-A'mal (ثواب الأعمال) and al-Majma' (والمجمع), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites ويل لكلّ همزة لمزة (Woe to every slanderer, defamer) in one of his obligatory prayers (فريضة من فرائضه), Allah will distance poverty from him, bring provision (الرزق) upon him, and ward off an evil death (ميتة السوء) from him."
